assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 1: 'Step 7 -- Concurrent Triage Detection for TC-8020'. Line 19: '1 issue returned.' Lines 21-22 show TC-8019 with status 'In Progress'. Line 27: 'CONCURRENT TRIAGE DETECTED on the same upstream component (quinn-proto)'.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 29-30: Warning table includes 'TC-8019 | In Progress | engineer-b@example.com'. Both the conflicting issue key (TC-8019) and assignee (engineer-b@example.com) are explicitly listed.

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 48-50: 'Option 1: Wait -- Pause execution until TC-8019's triage completes.' Lines 55-56: 'Option 2: Skip -- Skip Step 8 (remediation task creation) entirely for TC-8020.' Lines 74-76: 'Option 3: Proceed -- Create remediation tasks for TC-8020 as normal but add the concurrent-triage-overlap label.' All three options match the assertion.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 86: 'Then continue to Case A/B/C branching in Step 8 to create remediation tasks' (only after choosing Option 3). triage-outcome.md line 30: 'Step 7 (Concurrent Triage Detection) blocks remediation task creation.' Lines 65-96 show Case A and Case B are contingent on the Step 7 decision, confirming Step 7 runs before Step 8 branching.
